channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: What to know before the SJ Earthquakes’ MLS season opener vs. Real Salt Lake
item.description: Bruce Arena and the new-look San Jose Earthquakes open the MLS season on Saturday night at PayPal Park
item.dc:creator: Bay Area News Group, The Mercury News

item.title: Passion, politics and patriotism meld as Pakistan and India clash in cricket's fiercest rivalry
item.description: India and Pakistan are renewing their epic rivalry on the cricket field in front of an audience of hundreds of millions of people tuning in to catch the two arch rivals go head-to-head in Dubai in United Arab Emirates.
item.dc:creator: Sheikh Saaliq, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Michigan agrees to multiyear contract extension with coach Dusty May during successful debut season
item.description: ANN ARBOR, Mich. (AP) — Michigan announced a multiyear contract extension for Dusty May a couple hours before No. 14 Michigan State beat the 12th-ranked Wolverines 75-62 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 Nations Face-Off shatters expectations as a hockey showcase. The Milan Olympics are up next
item.description: Canada won the 4 Nations Face-Off by beating the United States for the championship in an overtime thriller capped off by Connor McDavid's memorable goal that will be on highlight reels for decades to come.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mark Williams' agent says center 'should have been a given an opportunity' to play for Lakers
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — The agent for Charlotte Hornets center Mark Williams says the Los Angeles Lakers shouldn't have failed his client’s physical examination, a move that ultimately resulted in the trade being rescinded after the deadline.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With Wembanyama's season now over, the Spurs and the basketball world wait for what's next
item.description: The French sports newspaper L’Equipe published a photo of Victor Wembanyama across the entirety of its front page on Friday, the San Antonio star looking down with his hands on his hips. “Coup D’Arret” was the headline in big, white letters. The translation, in this case, means Knockout Blow.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MLB, ESPN mutually parting ways on media rights deal after 2025 season: report
item.description: MLB commissioner Rob Manfred wrote in a letter to league owners that the media rights deal with ESPN has mutually been terminated after the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Rock says he will show up on 'SmackDown' with road to WrestleMania 41 underway
item.description: Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson announced on Thursday night that he will make his return to SmackDown in midst of the road to WrestleMania 41.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hockey fans criticize Wayne Gretzky as he represents Team Canada at 4 Nations final vs US
item.description: Wayne Gretzky as the honorary captain for Team Canada was widely panned on Thursday night ahead of their 4 Nations Face-Off final vs. the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-NBA star Michael Kidd-Gilchrist getting the word out about stuttering
item.description: Michael Kidd-Gilchrist, a former NBA forward and college basketball national champion, talked to Fox News Digital about his mission to raise awareness about stuttering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama to miss remainder of season after blood clot diagnosis
item.description: San Antonio star Victor Wembanyama's season is over after he was diagnosed with a blood clot in his right shoulder, a condition that will sideline one of the league's brightest stars and is the second massive health-related hit for the Spurs in a matter of months.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah State blasts San Jose State, 105-57
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah State basketball team got back on track following Sunday's heartbreaking loss to New Mexico with a resounding beatdown over San Jose State Wednesday night, 105-57.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ausar scores 21 as Utes run past K-State, 74-69
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Ezra Ausar had 21 points and Lawson Lovering posted a double-double to help Utah hold on for a 74-69 victory over Kansas State on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: William Byron speaks to NewsNation after Daytona 500 win
item.description: Byron made sure to celebrate his victory, sleeping for an hour before returning to Daytona International Speedway on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500 for Hendrick Motorsports
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla (AP) — William Byron fortuitously missed the ferocious wrecks down the stretch at the Daytona 500 that knocked out contenders racing for the checkered flag and left him with a repeat victory in sight.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500
item.description: Ninth in the No. 24 Chevrolet with one lap left, Byron was in the right place by racing near the outside wall in overtime to became the first back-to-back winner since Denny Hamlin in 2019-20.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Patrick Rodgers takes the lead at Torrey Pines as Scheffler and McIlroy falter
item.description: Rodgers was at his best Saturday when golf's best were faltering on a tough day at Torrey Pines, playing mistake-free until dumping a gap wedge into the water on the par-5 18th for his only bogey. That gave him a 4-under 68 and a one-shot lead going into the final round.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Basketball: Montgomery 58, Victory Christian Academy 44
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys hoops in Chula Vista on Tuesday night with Montgomery defeating Victory Christian Academy 58-44. Aztecs were lead by Senior JJ Sanchez. Montgomery was down at half and got going in the 2nd half for the double digit win.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5
